sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: The board changed after RIGHT. Let me check what changed by comparing the before and after frames.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed after RIGHT
before = last_transition.before_frame.segmentation
after = last_transition.after_frame.segmentation

# Compare node positions
def get_node_bbox(seg, nid):
    n = [x for x in seg['nodes'] if x['id'] == nid][0]
    b = n['boundary']
    rows = [p[0] for p in b]
    cols = [p[1] for p in b]
    return (min(rows), max(rows), min(cols), max(cols))

print("Changes after RIGHT:")
for n in after['nodes']:
    bid = get_node_bbox(before, n['id']) if n['id'] < len(before['nodes']) else None
    aid = get_node_bbox(after, n['id'])
    if bid and bid != aid:
        print(f"  id={n['id']} color={n['color']} moved: {bid} -> {aid}")
    elif not bid:
        print(f"  id={n['id']} color={n['color']} NEW: {aid}")

# Also check the top structure region
ascii_before = last_transition.before_frame.ascii.split('\n')
ascii_after = last_transition.after_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\nTop structure comparison (rows 17-22, cols 10-45):")
for r in range(17, 23):
    b = ascii_before[r][10:45]
    a = ascii_after[r][10:45]
    if b != a:
        print(f"  Row {r}: {b} -> {a}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: TOOL RESULT: python
sections.content:
Changes after RIGHT:

Top structure comparison (rows 17-22, cols 10-45):
  Row 19: BBbSSSSggggggggggggggggggggggbbSSBB -> BBbSSSSggggggggggggggggggggggSSbbBB
  Row 20: BBbSSSSggggggggggggggggggggggbbSSBB -> BBbSSSSggggggggggggggggggggggSSbbBB
  Row 21: BBbSSSSBBBBBBggggggggggggggggbbSSBB -> BBbSSSSBBBBBBggggggggggggggggSSbbBB
  Row 22: BBbbbbbBBBBBBggggggggggggggggbbSSBB -> BBbbbbbBBBBBBggggggggggggggggSSbbBB

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
So the RIGHT action moved the blue/sky-blue block on the right end of the horizontal bar. The `bbSS` pattern shifted right - the blue (b) moved from columns 39-40 to columns 41-42, and the sky blue (S) moved from columns 41-42 to columns 39-40. It seems like the right-end block is moving along the bar.
Let me understand the structure better:
- Left side: blue (b) 5x6 outer with sky blue (S) 4x4 inner - this seems stationary
- Middle: gray (g) bar
- Right side: blue (b) and sky blue (S) blocks that can move
Let me try more RIGHT actions to see how far it can go, and also try LEFT to see if it reverses.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# The right-end block moved right. Let me try LEFT to reverse it
action(['LEFT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
